item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: is his donger actually in a museum?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what would happen if you put him in the same room as SCP-049?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-4680 has been translated to Japanese.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I already gave this SCP the +1 after reading it the first time. Nicely written and put together (the latter being something that the SCP will not experience). What I have been wondering, after reading it, is how did this SCP do well? An immortal human with low-level regenerative abilities (just those powers being it), who happens to be an actual historical figure, seems to be an unimpressive concept alone; so how does the accompanying story behind it make up for it? Also is there an SCP that's involved with Pyotr and the circus?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh… I often wondered what Grigory would have to say about his schlong being in a museum if he had actually been immortal and survived the whole assassination attempt. Now I can picture him drinking miserably in a standard humanoid containment chamber :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rasputin lives for all eternity… without his dick.

God help me, I laughed.

Have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As much as I'd love to upvote this article that last line, I can't help but also hate it at the same time for thar same reason. I was hoping for a twist that'd tie him to Sarkicism somehow, or something that'd surprise the reader in a serious way.

Sorry, but -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was kind of considering writing a skip based on Rasputin and how difficult he was to kill. You beat me to it. Upvoted

ra ra
rasputin

There lived a certain man in Russia long ago
He was big and strong, in his eyes a flaming glow
Most people looked at him with terror and with fear
But to Moscow chicks he was such a lovely dear
He could preach the bible like a preacher
Full of ecstasy and fire
But he also was the kind of teacher
Women would desire
Ra ra Rasputin
Lover of the Russian queen
There was a cat that really was gone
Ra ra Rasputin
Russia's greatest love machine
It was a shame how he carried on
He ruled the Russian land and never mind the czar
But the cassock he danced really wunderbar
In all affairs of state he was the man to please
But he was real great when he had a girl to squeeze
For the queen he was no wheeler dealer
Though she'd heard the things he'd done
She believed he was a holy healer
Who would heal her son
Ra ra Rasputin
Lover of the Russian queen
There was a cat that really was gone
Ra ra Rasputin
Russia's greatest love machine
It was a shame how he carried on
But when his drinking and lusting and his hunger
For power became known to more and more people
The demands to do something about this outrageous
Man became louder and louder
This man's just got to go, declared his enemies
But the ladies begged, don't you try to do it, please
No doubt this Rasputin had lots of hidden charms
Though he was a brute they just fell into his arms
Then one night some men of higher standing
Set a trap, they're not to blame
Come to visit us they kept demanding
And he really came
Ra ra Rasputin
Lover of the Russian queen
They put some poison into his wine
Ra ra Rasputin
Russia's greatest love machine
He drank it all and said, I feel fine
Ra ra Rasputin
Lover of the Russian queen
They didn't quit, they wanted his head
Ra ra Rasputin
Russia's greatest love machine
And so they shot him 'til he was dead
Oh, those Russians

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really well done. Be careful what you wish for, Grigori.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this treats the guy like a joke. Notice that I am not calling the article itself a joke. I am not a fan. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The joke here is way too childish and the article itself does very little to provide something more. There is a lot of wasted potential with this immortal Raspurin concept. This article does remind me of song by Boney M, but it doesn't make it better for me. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Add "lolfoundation", plz

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Users of Russian Branch of SCP Foundation didn't like this object — it has only three upvotes and twenty five downvotes there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The probleme of this skip is that the character of Rasputin is shown as an extreme-lubric person. The character is caricatural, and if the begenning of the skip is good, in the next we have the sensation that the sex is the only way of life of Rasputin. But in reality, he was a very intelligent man ; and if his passion for the wives was probably historically correct, I don't think he was only thinking about this. That's why I downvote for this skip.
PS : sorry if my english is a little bit approximative, I'm comming from the french branch to critic the original text.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn you. The last note nearly made me lose my shit in the middle of class. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept of “immortal Rasputin” had huge potential. The last thing I expected was that it would be reduced to a loose recap of a Cracked.com article (which reeeeally exaggerates Rasputin's death) and an attempt to make a done-to-death joke slapped on top of it.
Of course, I wouldn't expect from Dave something on the level of Don Bluth or Mike Mignola, but this is just a load of bull. Maybe it’s just me, but it’s sad to see that such humor is appreciated here.
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait… seriously?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It really was a shame how he carried on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Show image
- Hide image

[https://orig00.deviantart.net/34cd/f/2018/069/d/0/goodheal_by_husii-dc5jfn5.png]

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Consider: sewing SCP-3313 onto him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
True, but it’s in a /jar/ in a museum. Preserved in formaldehyde, likely.

If anything, he’d probably just sleep with the jar as a teddy bear or summat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just have the Foundation sew his penis back. Surely, he can reattach severed extremities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pffffff.
Well, that punchline was hilarious.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was kind of curious how "Historical Figure, now as a SKIP" would work this time.

Then, the punchline.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

+1

item.title: Re: RA RA RASPUTIN
item.content:encoded:
WHY ARE YOU LAUGHING

HIS PENIS IS IN A MUSEUM

THIS IS A SERIOUS MATTER

item.title: RA RA RASPUTIN
item.content:encoded:
This ability is limited in that it cannot re-grow amputated extremities

MY PENIS IS IN A MUSEUM

I can't not upvote this, I'm laughing so hard

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1…when it comes to "sex",I can simply say this work is brilliant…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some insane Marxist book club

Got me giggling +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, if you could make it work, i’d be all for it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4680 is to be provided with no more than five bottles of inexpensive red wine per day, upon request. …this has proven to be the most effective method of pacification, sedation, and maintenance of its mental health during containment

This got me laughing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have an anomalous circus freak, and you don't mention the Circus of the Disquieting?

I…you…but…ah…oh.

+1.

In all seriousness, if you wouldn't object I'd be seriously tempted to write a Herman Fuller format where Rasputin springs this indestructible man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will politely disagree.

I knew they were talking about his penis at the 'extremities don't grow back' part, and I think the burst of raw emotion makes it all the funnier to those who are familiar with Rasputin's legendary penis and are waiting for the punchline to hit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neutral voting. RaSCPutin is a clever idea but the ending would have much more punch if the Foundation noted down that his dick was in the museum, instead of Rasputin writing that down himself. The restraint is what makes the punchline hit harder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
UNFORGIVABLE
NICHOLAS, YOU WHORE
I HOPE LENIN SKINS YOU ALIVE IN HELL
MY PENIS IS IN A MUSEUM

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Normally, I despise short, funny skips

But screw it, I'll make an exception here, I laughed

+1

item.title: Russia's Greatest Love Machine!
item.content:encoded: Image is public domain.
